heading: Safe conduct violation
label: 1545 Safe conduct violation
text: Whoever violates any safe conduct or passport duly obtained and issued under authority of the United States shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Foreign Relations and Intercourse (R.S. 4062).section 251 of title 22The punishment provision was rewritten to permit the alternative of a fine of not more than $2,000 or imprisonment, or both, instead of imprisonment and fine “at the discretion of the court”, to conform with other sections embracing offenses of comparable gravity.Minor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments1994—, which directed the amendment of this section by substituting “under this title” for “not more than $2,000”, could not be executed because the words “not more than $2,000” did not appear in text subsequent to amendment by . See below.Pub. L. 103–322, § 330016(1)(I)Pub. L. 103–322, § 130009(a)(3), substituted “under this title, imprisoned not more than 10 years” for “not more than $2,000 or imprisoned not more than three years”.Pub. L. 103–322, § 130009(a)(3)
